go_no_go_recommendation: Proceed with Caution. While the project addresses a critical issue of digital sovereignty, several immediate actions are required to mitigate significant risks. Securing a physical workspace, establishing a secure code repository, and setting up a financial tracking system are essential for project initiation and risk management. If these actions are not completed successfully within the specified timeframes, the project should be re-evaluated. Proceeding without these foundational elements would expose the project to unacceptable operational, security, and financial risks. The project should not proceed to Phase 2 until these actions are completed.
combined_summary: The three most critical, immediate actions are: 1) Secure a physical workspace in Copenhagen by 2026-03-15 17:00, as this is essential for a secure project base and team collaboration. 2) Establish a secure code repository with access controls by 2026-03-11 17:00, as this protects intellectual property and prevents unauthorized code modifications. 3) Set up a dedicated accounting system to track project expenditures against the budget by 2026-03-11 17:00, as this is needed to monitor spending and prevent cost overruns. These actions mitigate operational, security, and financial risks, respectively, and are crucial for project initiation.

feedback.title: Secure Physical Base Immediately
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Secure a lease or agreement for a physical workspace in Copenhagen by 2026-03-15 17:00. This workspace must meet the requirements for security-sensitive digital identity research, development, and stakeholder meetings, as outlined in 'assumptions.md'. This is needed to provide a secure and appropriate environment for the project team.
*   If a suitable location cannot be secured by 2026-03-15 17:00, identify at least three alternative temporary co-working spaces that meet basic security requirements (e.g., secure Wi-Fi, private meeting rooms) by 2026-03-18 17:00. This is needed to ensure the project can commence without delay.
*   Document the security features of the chosen location, including access control measures, data encryption protocols, and incident response plans, by 2026-03-22 17:00. This is needed to ensure compliance with security requirements.
*   Purchase at least 5 physical locks and keys for the office, and assign them to the core team members by 2026-03-22 17:00. This is needed to ensure physical security of the office.

feedback.title: Define Demonstrator Technical Specifications
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Define the precise technical specifications for both the mobile-oriented (FIDO2/WebAuthn on GrapheneOS) and browser-based/hardware-token-based fallback demonstrators by 2026-03-15 17:00. This is needed to provide a clear roadmap for the technical team.
*   Specify the exact versions of GrapheneOS, FIDO2 libraries, WebAuthn APIs, and OpenID Connect protocols to be used in the demonstrators by 2026-03-15 17:00. This is needed to ensure compatibility and avoid versioning conflicts.
*   Outline the data structures and API endpoints for the simulated Danish public-sector identity-provider environment by 2026-03-15 17:00. This is needed to create a realistic testing environment.
*   Create a detailed Gantt chart outlining the tasks, dependencies, and timelines for demonstrator development, including milestones for prototyping, testing, and external review, by 2026-03-18 17:00. This is needed to track progress and manage resources effectively.

feedback.title: Establish Financial Tracking System
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Set up a dedicated accounting system (e.g., Xero, QuickBooks) to track all project expenditures against the allocated budget of 10.5 million DKK by 2026-03-11 17:00. This is needed to monitor spending and prevent cost overruns.
*   Create a detailed budget breakdown for Phase One (3.0 million DKK), allocating funds to specific categories such as personnel, travel, software, hardware, and institutional overhead by 2026-03-11 17:00. This is needed to ensure resources are allocated efficiently.
*   Establish a process for tracking and approving all invoices and expenses, including required documentation and approval workflows, by 2026-03-11 17:00. This is needed to maintain financial transparency and accountability.
*   Identify at least three potential sources of EU or private grant funding for digital sovereignty or open-source initiatives by 2026-03-15 17:00, and assign responsibility for preparing and submitting grant applications. This is needed to supplement the existing budget and ensure long-term financial sustainability.

feedback.title: Draft Initial Stakeholder Engagement Plan
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Identify at least 10 key stakeholders from civil-society organizations, privacy advocates, academic researchers, security experts, contingency-planning stakeholders, and members of Folketinget's digital policy committee by 2026-03-11 17:00. This is needed to build a coalition of support.
*   Draft a preliminary stakeholder engagement plan outlining communication channels, meeting schedules, and messaging strategies for each stakeholder group by 2026-03-11 17:00. This is needed to ensure effective communication and build consensus.
*   Prepare a briefing document summarizing the project's objectives, risks, and potential benefits, tailored to the interests of each stakeholder group, by 2026-03-15 17:00. This is needed to communicate the project's value proposition effectively.
*   Schedule initial meetings with at least three key stakeholders by 2026-03-18 17:00 to introduce the project and solicit their feedback. This is needed to build relationships and identify potential allies.

feedback.title: Implement Secure Code Repository
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Establish a secure code repository (e.g., GitLab, GitHub with 2FA enabled) with access controls limiting code access to authorized personnel only by 2026-03-11 17:00. This is needed to protect the project's intellectual property and prevent unauthorized modifications.
*   Implement code review processes requiring at least two independent reviewers to approve all code changes before they are merged into the main branch by 2026-03-15 17:00. This is needed to identify and prevent security vulnerabilities.
*   Configure automated security scanning tools (e.g., SonarQube, Snyk) to automatically scan the code repository for common security vulnerabilities and coding errors by 2026-03-18 17:00. This is needed to proactively identify and address security risks.
*   Enforce a strict policy of using only approved and vetted open-source libraries and dependencies, and regularly update these dependencies to address known security vulnerabilities by 2026-03-22 17:00. This is needed to minimize the risk of introducing vulnerabilities through third-party code.

feedback.title: Define Data Handling and Privacy Protocols
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Develop a comprehensive data handling and privacy protocol that complies with Danish and EU data protection regulations (GDPR) by 2026-03-15 17:00. This is needed to ensure the project handles personal data responsibly and legally.
*   Implement data encryption at rest and in transit for all sensitive data, including cryptographic key management procedures, by 2026-03-18 17:00. This is needed to protect data from unauthorized access.
*   Establish a clear data retention policy specifying how long data will be stored and when it will be securely deleted by 2026-03-22 17:00. This is needed to minimize the risk of data breaches and comply with data protection regulations.
*   Implement a robust access control system that restricts access to sensitive data based on the principle of least privilege, granting users only the minimum necessary access rights by 2026-03-25 17:00. This is needed to prevent unauthorized access to sensitive data.

feedback.title: Establish Incident Response Plan
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Develop a detailed incident response plan outlining the steps to be taken in the event of a security breach or data leak by 2026-03-18 17:00. This is needed to ensure a swift and effective response to security incidents.
*   Establish a clear chain of command and communication protocols for reporting and managing security incidents by 2026-03-22 17:00. This is needed to ensure timely and coordinated responses to security incidents.
*   Conduct regular security audits and penetration testing to identify and address potential vulnerabilities in the project's systems and infrastructure by 2026-03-25 17:00. This is needed to proactively identify and mitigate security risks.
*   Implement a system for logging and monitoring all security-related events, including access attempts, code changes, and system errors, by 2026-03-29 17:00. This is needed to detect and investigate security incidents.

feedback.title: Define Regulatory Compliance Framework
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Identify all relevant Danish and EU regulations related to digital identity, data protection, and cybersecurity, including eIDAS, NSIS, and GDPR, by 2026-03-11 17:00. This is needed to ensure the project complies with all applicable legal requirements.
*   Establish a compliance framework outlining the specific steps the project will take to comply with each relevant regulation by 2026-03-15 17:00. This is needed to provide a clear roadmap for compliance.
*   Assign responsibility for monitoring regulatory changes and updating the compliance framework accordingly by 2026-03-18 17:00. This is needed to ensure the project remains compliant over time.
*   Engage legal counsel to review the project's compliance framework and provide guidance on any legal or regulatory issues by 2026-03-22 17:00. This is needed to ensure the project's compliance efforts are legally sound.
